risks: ADLS Gen2 (HNS-enabled) storage used as ML workspace default storage is unsupported per Microsoft documentation
Storage networkAcls defaultAction is 'Allow' — data lake accessible from all networks despite 'secure' goal
ML workspace publicNetworkAccess set to 'Enabled' — training endpoints publicly accessible
Standard_LRS provides no geo or zone redundancy for training data and model artifacts
No RBAC role assignments defined for the managed identity on Storage, Key Vault, or ACR
No private endpoints configured for Key Vault, Storage, or ML workspace
Basic ACR SKU does not support private link — limits network isolation options
correctionsRecommended: Split into two storage accounts: a non-HNS StorageV2 as ML workspace default, and an HNS-enabled ADLS Gen2 as a mounted datastore for training data
Set storage networkAcls.defaultAction to 'Deny' and configure VNet service endpoints or private endpoints
Add explicit RBAC role assignments: Storage Blob Data Contributor, Key Vault Crypto Officer, AcrPull for the managed identity
Consider Standard_ZRS or Standard_GRS for storage durability in production
Add resource tags (environment, project, cost-center) for operational governance
Evaluate switching ML workspace managed network to AllowOnlyApprovedOutbound for tighter egress control
Upgrade ACR to Premium SKU if private link is required for network isolation
hardDependencies: ML workspace storageAccount references HNS-enabled storage (isHnsEnabled: true) — Azure ML does not support ADLS Gen2 as default workspace storage
